mission.title: spin: I’d really like to opt-in to Wasm features
mission.description: I’d really like to opt-in to Wasm features. Community-requested feature.

steps.title: Check current spin setup
steps.description:
Verify your spin version and configuration:
```bash
spin version
```
This feature requires a working spin installation.

steps.title: Review spin configuration
steps.description: Review the relevant spin configuration: With a significant amount of effort I was able to write ‘hello world‘ in Kotlin and run it on Spin! Hooray! One

steps.title: Apply the fix for I’d really like to opt-in to Wasm features
steps.description:
How about this:

- Add a new flag `spin up --enable-unstable-feature <X>`
- When this flag is present, print a loud warning along the lines of "You have enabled unstable features which might go away at any time, bring a plague of locusts to your land, etc."
- Let's start conservatively and just add the 4 features  needs. I think its fine for this to be very verbose, e.g.:
  ```
  $ spin up --enable-unstable-feature wasm_gc \
            --enable-unstable-feature wasm_reference_types \

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected.
Confirm the feature described in "I’d really like to opt-in to Wasm features" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary:
How about this:

- Add a new flag `spin up --enable-unstable-feature <X>`
- When this flag is present, print a loud warning along the lines of "You have enabled unstable features which might go away at any time, bring a plague of locusts to your land, etc."
- Let's start conservatively and just add the 4 features  needs.

prerequisites.description: A working spin installation or development environment.
